title: Incorrectly Implemented Euclidean Algorithm for GCD

statement.markdown: The Euclidean algorithm is a method for computing the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two integers. The GCD of two integers a and b is defined as the largest positive integer that divides both a and b without leaving a remainder. Given two integers a and b, your function should return their GCD, but the provided implementation contains a logical bug.
statement.inputFormat: Two integers a and b in range 1-10000.
statement.outputFormat: The GCD of the given integers a and b.
statement.notes: The time complexity of the Euclidean algorithm is O(log min(a, b)). Do not use any library functions.

hints.text: Use the Euclidean algorithm to find the GCD.
Be careful with the sign of the result.

editorial.title: Euclidean Algorithm for GCD
editorial.intuition: The Euclidean algorithm is a method for computing the GCD of two integers by repeatedly replacing the larger number with the remainder of the division of that number by the smaller number.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute-force approach: Try all numbers from 1 to n to check if they divide both a and b without leaving a remainder.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the Euclidean algorithm using a while loop.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: By the definition of the Euclidean algorithm, the GCD of a and b is the largest positive integer that divides both a and b without leaving a remainder.
editorial.dryRun: Consider an example where a = 21 and b = 15. The GCD of 21 and 15 is 3.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case when a or b is zero.

starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int findGCD(int a, int b) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def findGCD(self, a: int, b: int) -> int: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int findGCD(int a, int b) { if (a == 0) return b; return b % a == 0 ? a : b; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int findGCD(int a, int b) { if (a == 0) return b; return b % a == 0 ? a : b; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def findGCD(self, a: int, b: int) -> int: if a == 0: return b; return b % a == 0 and a or b
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function findGCD(a, b) { if (a == 0) return b; return b % a == 0 ? a : b }
